name: Baked Fish Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the potatoes into chunks and place them in a saucepan with enough water to cover, adding a little salt if desired. Heat until just about to boil.
2. Meanwhile, lay the fish on a plate or dish to cover the saucepan and cover it with a second plate, lid, or foil. Put the fish on the pan when the water is boiling, then reduce the heat so the water does not froth over. Cook the potatoes until tender (10-15 minutes if the chunks are fairly small). Set aside, uncovered.
3. Drain and mash the potatoes, beating well until smooth. Mix in the butter, milk, lemon peel, parsley, and anchovy extract (if used).
4. Preheat oven to 400°F.
5. Grease a quick-cake dish or baking pan. Pour any cooking juices from the fish into the potato mixture. Flake the fish off the skin, discard any bones, and add it to the potatoes. Mix well, adding seasoning to taste.
6. Use a large spoon to place the fish mixture in a mound on the prepared dish or pan; do not flatten it until it is all in mounded up. Flatten the top of the mixture slightly, then sprinkle with a little flour. Use a spatula to slice into a neat circle, about 7 inches across. Pat the sides and top with a knife to ensure an even and neatly shaped cake.
7. Drizzle the oil over the fish cake and bake for about 30 minutes, until well browned. Cut into wedges to serve.
